2603.19667.title: Toward High-Fidelity Visual Reconstruction: From EEG-Based Conditioned Generation to Joint-Modal Guided Rebuilding
2603.19667.paper_summary: Human visual reconstruction aims to reconstruct fine-grained visual stimuli based on subject-provided descriptions and corresponding neural signals. As a widely adopted modality, Electroencephalography (EEG) captures rich visual cognition information, encompassing complex spatial relationships and chromatic details within scenes. However, current approaches are deeply coupled with an alignment framework that forces EEG features to align with text or image semantic representation. The dependency may condense the rich spatial and chromatic details in EEG that achieved mere conditioned image generation rather than high-fidelity visual reconstruction. To address this limitation, we propose a novel Joint-Modal Visual Reconstruction (JMVR) framework. It treats EEG and text as independent modalities for joint learning to preserve EEG-specific information for reconstruction. It further employs a multi-scale EEG encoding strategy to capture both fine- and coarse-grained features, alongside image augmentation to enhance the recovery of perceptual details. Extensive experiments on the THINGS-EEG dataset demonstrate that JMVR achieves SOTA performance against six baseline methods, specifically exhibiting superior capabilities in modeling spatial structure and chromatic fidelity.
2603.19667.paper_summary_zh: 人類視覺重建旨在根據受試者提供的描述和相應的神經信號重建精細的視覺刺激。作為一種廣泛採用的模式，腦電圖（EEG）捕捉豐富的視覺認知信息，包括場景中的複雜空間關係和色彩細節。然而，目前的方法與一個對齊框架深度耦合，迫使EEG特徵與文本或圖像語義表示對齊。這種依賴可能會壓縮EEG中的豐富空間和色彩細節，僅達到條件圖像生成，而非高保真視覺重建。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了一種新穎的聯合模態視覺重建（JMVR）框架。它將EEG和文本視為獨立的模態進行聯合學習，以保留EEG特定的信息以進行重建。它進一步採用多尺度EEG編碼策略來捕捉細粒度和粗粒度特徵，並結合圖像增強來提高感知細節的恢復。對THINGS-EEG數據集的廣泛實驗表明，JMVR在六種基線方法中達到了SOTA性能，特別是在建模空間結構和色彩保真度方面展現了優越的能力。
2603.19667.author: Zhijian Gong et.al.
2603.19667.authors: Zhijian Gong, Tianren Yao, Wenjia Dong, Xueyuan Xu
